title: The Luck Of The Fryrish

jokes.text: Squeeze one out for America, honey.
jokes.punchline: Double entendre mixing patriotic language with crude bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay mixing childbirth with crude humor and patriotism. Economy is good but relies on cheap double entendre.

jokes.text: Keep it down, it's the 9th inning.
jokes.punchline: Father prioritizes baseball over childbirth
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing skewed priorities. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: You saying my son's a commie?
jokes.setup: Doctor mentions baby has red hair
jokes.punchline: Father immediately assumes political implications of hair color
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing paranoid Cold War mentality. Great integration and originality.

jokes.text: This is the happiest day of my life. - Here's your baby. - Okay, thanks.
jokes.setup: Father declares happiest day of life
jokes.punchline: Completely casual 'okay, thanks' when handed his newborn
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with excellent economy. The casual dismissal after emotional declaration is beautifully structured.

jokes.text: You pick. I picked dinner.
jokes.setup: Deciding what to name the baby
jokes.punchline: Treating baby naming like choosing a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about relationship dynamics. Good economy but fairly conventional.

jokes.text: I was thinking of Philip, after those screwdrivers.
jokes.punchline: Named after screwdrivers, not a person
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing blue-collar priorities. Solid character integration.

jokes.text: Me Philip! Me Philip!
jokes.punchline: Toddler's jealous tantrum in caveman speak
jokes.explanation: Basic sibling rivalry humor. Functional but not particularly original or crafted.

jokes.text: All the way back to minuteman Yancy Fry... who blasted commies in the American Revolution.
jokes.punchline: Anachronistically fighting communists in 1776
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical anachronism joke. Shows character's political obsession while being genuinely clever about timeline.

jokes.text: Philip, until I find a suitable model of an ICBM... you'll have to make do fearing this toy spacecraft.
jokes.punchline: Teaching infant to fear nuclear weapons via toy rocket
jokes.explanation: Dark and brilliant character comedy. Perfect integration of Cold War paranoia with parenting. Highly quotable and earned.

jokes.text: To hell with your baby. I need those shoes.
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'baby needs new shoes' while gambling
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses the baby, claims he needs the shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment. Excellent integration of his selfish nature with gambling cliche subversion.

jokes.text: No fair! You changed the outcome by measuring it.
jokes.setup: Quantum finish horse race being measured
jokes.punchline: Complaining about quantum mechanics observer effect in gambling context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant science humor applying quantum physics to gambling. Highly original and well-structured, very quotable.

jokes.text: I'll tell you when my horse finishes. - Bad.
jokes.setup: Asked how he did betting, says he'll tell when horse finishes
jokes.punchline: Immediate deadpan 'Bad'
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and economy. Simple but effective timing-based humor.

jokes.text: Hey, baby, ever do it in a suitcase?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate pickup line from tiny jockey
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor playing on jockey size. Character-specific but relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: I meant tongue luck.
jokes.setup: Asking Leela for a kiss for good luck
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he meant French kissing
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay relying on crude humor. Low originality and earned value.

jokes.text: I've run over black cats that were luckier than me.
jokes.punchline: Comparing his luck to cats he's killed while driving
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good economy and structure. Original take on bad luck complaints, highly quotable.

jokes.text: Get your hot horse-burgers, horse-fries, horse-cakes and shakes.
jokes.punchline: Everything is made from horses
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept. Sets up running gag effectively with decent economy.

jokes.text: All our horses are 100% horse-fed for that double juiced-in goodness.
jokes.punchline: Cannibalistic horses and made-up marketing speak
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag with wordplay. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'll have a cholesterol-free omelet with horse-beaters.
jokes.punchline: Egg beaters become horse beaters
jokes.explanation: Continues gag with wordplay twist. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: How can I horse you, sir?
jokes.punchline: Help becomes horse
jokes.explanation: Weakest in the running gag. Forced wordplay without much craft.

jokes.text: Horse Pepsi okay? - Neigh!
jokes.punchline: No becomes horse sound
jokes.explanation: Best of the horse gag sequence. Good structure and economy with the pun payoff.

jokes.text: Dear Horse God, I don't usually pray. Sometimes I doubt you even exist.
jokes.punchline: Praying to Horse God with honest doubt
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist prayer with honest character moment. Original concept with solid structure.

jokes.text: Don't do that. What are you...? It's contagious.
jokes.setup: Other horses start dancing after one dances
jokes.punchline: Fry thinks dancing is literally contagious
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag with Fry's naive interpretation. Well-balanced across all craft elements.

jokes.text: That'll teach those horses to take drugs.
jokes.setup: Horse wins by entire racetrack length
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to horse doping as lesson learned
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about horse racing reality. Good economy.

jokes.text: That'll teach those horses to take drugs.
jokes.punchline: Implies other horses were on performance enhancing drugs
jokes.explanation: Amy's deadpan delivery makes absurd explanation seem reasonable

jokes.text: I may not know about horses, but I know lots about doing anything for $1.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to degrading himself for small amounts
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Fry's desperation and low standards. Well-integrated and earned.

jokes.text: If you think bad luck can defeat me, you don't know my name is Philip J...
jokes.setup: Fry defiantly declaring he can't be defeated
jokes.punchline: Gets crushed mid-sentence, proving the opposite
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing. Excellent structure with immediate ironic reversal.

jokes.text: That is one unlucky guy.
jokes.setup: Fry gets crushed by falling object
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of obvious situation
jokes.explanation: Standard understatement humor. Good economy but low originality and integration.

jokes.text: Kareem has the sky hook, but Philip J. Fry has the space hook.
jokes.setup: Playing basketball and referencing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar
jokes.punchline: Futuristic version of famous basketball move
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay updating classic basketball reference. Balanced but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Hey! That's my patented space hook. You stole it. - You're not the president of it.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical defense about not being 'president' of a move
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with absurd child logic. Shows their competitive relationship early.

jokes.text: Holy camolie! A seven-leaf clover.
jokes.setup: Finding something in the basketball court
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'cannoli' while finding impossible clover
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with malapropism. Sets up central plot device with Fry's distinctive speech pattern.

jokes.text: I'm dying of old age!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction with impossible medical condition
jokes.explanation: Standard child overreaction humor. Good economy but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Someone's in a good mode.
jokes.punchline: Robot says 'mode' instead of 'mood'
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific language humor. Excellent character integration showing Bender's mechanical nature.

jokes.text: What happened to this clover? - I hid it in a secret place and never told anyone. Not even Scruffy.
jokes.punchline: Claims he told no one, then immediately mentions telling Scruffy
jokes.explanation: Good character inconsistency humor. Shows Fry's scattered thinking patterns.

jokes.text: Dirt doesn't need luck.
jokes.setup: Fry worrying clover might be giving luck to dirt
jokes.punchline: Leela's practical dismissal of the concern
jokes.explanation: Good Leela straight-woman response. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: My full name is Bender Bending Rodriguez.
jokes.setup: Asked if bending is his middle name
jokes.punchline: Reveals his literally functional naming convention
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing robot naming logic. Perfect integration and good rewatch value.

jokes.text: He was fun.
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual, heartless dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment. Excellent economy and character integration showing his callousness.

jokes.text: The city that inspired a casino in Las Vegas.
jokes.punchline: Reversing the usual relationship between original and copy
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of expectation. Good structure and economy with original concept.

jokes.text: Howard Stern is overrated.
jokes.setup: Fry saying he can finally do crazy stuff in empty New York
jokes.punchline: Most rebellious thing is mild celebrity criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Fry's tameness. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: New York is so burned. Remember when Giuliani cracked down on jaywalking? Well, Rudy, how do you like this action?
jokes.setup: Leela getting rebellious in empty New York
jokes.punchline: Her idea of rebellion is jaywalking with political commentary
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's rule-following nature. Solid integration but slightly verbose.

jokes.text: Cosmic F. - Outer space. - The moonwalk. The robot. The zero G.
jokes.punchline: Space-themed name with literal space dance moves
jokes.explanation: Good character setup showing young Fry's interests. Solid integration with space theme.

jokes.text: Deep Space?! - You totally ripped off my routine.
jokes.punchline: Immediate accusation over similar space theme
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry moment. Strong character integration showing their competitive dynamic.

jokes.text: The septuple head-spin! - He's just scratching. No one's done more than a quadruple.
jokes.punchline: Seven head-spins when four is the maximum
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor with absurdist element. Solid structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Noticeably F.A.T., rewind the tape.
jokes.setup: MC doesn't believe the septuple spin happened
jokes.punchline: Demanding video evidence using the DJ
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation. Solid structure showing Fry's confidence.

jokes.text: 71st Street? Never heard of it.
jokes.setup: Reading street sign in his old neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Doesn't recognize street he presumably lived on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's obliviousness. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: That feels funny!
jokes.punchline: Obvious reaction to spinning motion
jokes.explanation: Weak physical comedy. Low originality and earned value, stating the obvious.

jokes.text: This is the Brooklyn-bound B-train, stopping wherever I feel like.
jokes.punchline: Completely unprofessional and arbitrary service
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about public transportation. Decent economy and structure.

jokes.text: That's the bench where I found shirts. We used to light that hydrant on fire!
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical activities presented as normal memories
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Fry's confused/poor upbringing. Original and well-integrated.

jokes.text: Was it poorly xeroxed? - Oh, yeah.
jokes.punchline: Confirming stereotype about low-budget political materials
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about political activism stereotypes. Solid across metrics.

jokes.text: The comedians were right. This is different from L.A.
jokes.punchline: Reference to standard comedy material about NY vs LA
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to comedy clichés. Decent economy but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I mutated from him.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of literal genetic mutation
jokes.explanation: Good futuristic humor with excellent economy. Treats mutation casually for comedic effect.

jokes.text: I'm teaching. - He seemed nice. - Sure, when he's sober.
jokes.punchline: Dark reveal about alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Standard dark humor reveal. Good structure and economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Man, Father Time really took a bat to this place.
jokes.setup: Looking at his deteriorated childhood home
jokes.punchline: Violent metaphor for aging/decay
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good metaphor. Balanced across all craft elements.

jokes.text: What's happening? Did Kremlin Joe let fly with the nukes?
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes nuclear war
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Cold War paranoia. Perfect integration with established character traits.

jokes.text: He better keep his hands off these bananas. Gonna need them when the radiation turns us all into monkeys.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre survival logic about needing bananas for monkey transformation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic with perfect character integration. Highly original and quotable, excellent earned humor.

jokes.text: I can't wait until I'm old enough to feel ways about stuff.
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of developing emotional maturity
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line showing child's innocent perspective on growing up. Highly original, perfectly structured, extremely quotable.

jokes.text: Yet there was no nuclear war. - What a waste.
jokes.setup: Dad built bomb shelter but no nuclear war happened
jokes.punchline: Leela casually dismissing the positive outcome as waste
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Leela's practical perspective. Excellent economy and solid structure.

jokes.text: The Achilles' heel of the Ronco design is its structural resonance frequency.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining how to open stuck vault
jokes.punchline: Over-technical explanation for shoddily-made product flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment using scientific jargon for infomercial product

jokes.text: You mean breakfast club sandwich?
jokes.punchline: Bender interprets everything as food-related
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's limited cultural knowledge. Strong integration with his personality.

jokes.text: Except Sports, by Huey Lewis.
jokes.setup: Everything else in vault held up okay
jokes.punchline: Reference to famously bad Huey Lewis album with sports pun
jokes.explanation: Decent music reference humor. Good economy but requires specific cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: Good Lord! He ditched his goofy name and stole mine.
jokes.punchline: Calling 'Yancy' goofy while named 'Philip J. Fry'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor about name preferences. Strong character integration and solid structure.

jokes.text: You opened the vault, and the clover was gone? - Nothing in there but the best music of the 1980s.
jokes.punchline: Fry dismisses 80s music as worthless
jokes.explanation: Decent generational humor about music preferences. Good economy but fairly standard.

jokes.text: Survivor, Pat Benatar, The Scorpions. The list goes on.
jokes.punchline: Treating great 80s bands as worthless
jokes.explanation: Standard music reference humor. Functional but not particularly crafted or original.

jokes.text: It also opened my calendar to Friday and ordered some french fries.
jokes.setup: Browser heard word 'Fry' and found movie
jokes.punchline: Overly literal AI making unwanted purchases
jokes.explanation: Good futuristic humor about AI misinterpreting commands. Original concept with solid structure.

jokes.text: Good, we're going grave robbing. - I'll get my kit.
jokes.setup: Planning to get clover from grave
jokes.punchline: Bender casually has grave robbing equipment ready
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment. Excellent integration showing his criminal nature is always ready.

jokes.text: Yancy, that tux got me through Nam in style.
jokes.setup: Mr. Fry lending tuxedo to son
jokes.punchline: Wore formal wear during Vietnam War
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of formal wear in combat that fits character's priorities

jokes.text: He may be missing, but his crap sure isn't.
jokes.setup: Looking through Fry's belongings at wedding
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about missing brother's possessions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Yancy's practical but insensitive nature. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Breakfast Club. This will clear the room after the reception.
jokes.punchline: Planning to use it to end wedding reception
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier moment. Good economy but fairly standard wedding humor.

jokes.text: Now no one will be able to say I don't own John Larroquette's spine.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre priority about celebrity remains
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender moment with absurd priorities. Highly original, perfect character integration, very quotable.

jokes.text: He wasn't original. He wasn't a Martian. He wasn't Philip. He wasn't a 'the.'
jokes.setup: Fry ranting about his brother's epitaph
jokes.punchline: Escalates to arguing about grammar articles
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that ends absurdly with grammatical complaints

jokes.text: You're twice the 'the' he ever was.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical but supportive wordplay comfort
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Leela's supportive but logical nature. Original concept with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Really? Son, I'm naming you Philip J. Fry... in honor of my little brother, who I miss every day.
jokes.punchline: Reveals touching reason behind name choice
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff that recontextualizes entire episode. Excellent structure and character integration, deeply earned through episode buildup.

jokes.text: Pay dirt! I got the clover, plus his wedding ring. Sorry, ladies, I'm taken.
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding wedding ring significance while robbing relative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's obliviousness. Solid structure with ironic context.

jokes.text: Hey, Fry, you want me to smack the corpse up a little?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to be helpful to distraught Fry
jokes.punchline: Offers to desecrate grave as friendship gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - offers violence as comfort, completely missing emotional context

jokes.text: Okay, grab a shovel. I'm one skull short of a Mouseketeer reunion.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they help with grave robbing
jokes.punchline: Needs specific number of skulls for Disney reference
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark humor mixing Disney innocence with macabre collecting. Highly original, perfect Bender integration, extremely quotable.

tmdb_overview: After a string of bad luck, Fry ventures into the decaying ruins of Old New York to regain his lucky seven-leaf clover from his childhood, only to find that his brother Yancy Fry had stolen not only the clover, but Fry's identity as well. Fry sets out to exhume his brother's body, but discovers the startling truth about Yancy instead.
